name: Global Intelligence — News Source Registry
notes: Machine-readable registry of the world's important news media, consumed by the Global Intelligence pipelines and displayed on the site.
RSS URLs are best-known candidates and are UNVERIFIED until the pipeline build validates each one; prefer RSS/official APIs over scraping.
Pipelines must summarize in our own words and attribute/link to sources — never republish article text.
state_affiliated=true marks government-funded or state-controlled outlets; the AI curation layer must weight/flag these for bias.
This is v0.1 — coverage will grow toward all countries/regions.
v0.2: validated/fixed 18 feeds; sources without native RSS use Google News per-site search RSS (public method).

v0.3: added 7 African sources (Ethiopia, Ghana, Tanzania, Uganda, Morocco, Zimbabwe, Rwanda) — round 1 of closing the continent-coverage gap identified when auditing source geographic distribution (Africa was 4/78 sources for ~18% of world population). All 7 feeds independently verified to return real, non-empty content before commit.

v0.4: added the first Nordic sources (DR Denmark, SVT Sweden, Aftenposten Norway, Yle Finland — all previously zero) and the first Central Asian sources (The Astana Times Kazakhstan, Gazeta.uz Uzbekistan — previously zero). Several of these are native-language (da/sv/no/fi) rather than English — deliberately kept native, matching the existing precedent of Spanish/Portuguese/German/French/Italian sources already in this registry; the app has no translation layer, native-language headlines display as-is, same as those existing entries.

v0.5: added Colombia (El Tiempo), Chile (La Tercera), Peru (El Comercio), Venezuela (El Nacional) — the Americas region was 87% USA/Canada before this; South America beyond Brazil/Argentina was entirely absent.
v0.6: added Iran (Iran International), Iraq (Rudaw), UAE (The National), Jordan (Jordan Times), Lebanon (L'Orient Today) — Middle East previously only had Qatar/Saudi Arabia/Israel(x2)/Turkey/Egypt.

v0.7: added the first Francophone African sources — TSA (Algeria), Radio Okapi (DR Congo), SenePlus (Senegal), Journal du Cameroun — zero French-language African source existed before this despite French being an official language in ~21 African countries. Verification note: the Google-News-per-site-search method returned garbage/placeholder results for 2 of these domains (radiookapi.net returned unrelated spam-like titles; seneplus.com/fratmat.info returned only the bare source name with no headline) — switched to each site's own native /feed or /rss.xml instead, confirmed with real, plausible headlines before adding. A 5th candidate (Fraternité Matin, Ivory Coast) had no working feed via either method and was dropped rather than added with unverified/low quality.
v0.8: added Philippines (Philippine Daily Inquirer), Malaysia (The Star), Sri Lanka (Daily Mirror), Afghanistan (TOLOnews) — closing the remaining Southeast/South Asia gaps.

v0.9: added Sudan (Sudan Tribune), Angola (ANGOP), Mali (Malijet), Mozambique (Club of Mozambique), Madagascar (Madagascar Tribune) — closing the last of the previously-documented Africa gap list. ANGOP is Angola's state news agency (state_affiliated=true, same precedent as Xinhua/TASS/CCTV already in this registry). For Mali, Journal du Mali's feed and Studio Tamani's feed were tested first but skewed heavily toward sports/soft-lifestyle content; Malijet's native rss.xml returned substantive hard news (security operations, flood-prevention infrastructure, regional diplomacy) and was used instead — a reminder that the first plausible-looking candidate for a country isn't always the best one available.

v1.0: pivoted from breadth (new countries) to depth — added a second, independent source for 4 major countries that had only ever had one: Pakistan (The Express Tribune), Indonesia (Tempo.co), Nigeria (Vanguard), Turkey (Hürriyet Daily News, mainstream/private — pairs with the existing TRT World, which is state-affiliated=true, so Turkey now has both a state and an independent voice). A 5th candidate, Mexico, was attempted but every tested source (Milenio, Excelsior, Animal Político, Proceso, Infobae, El Universal via Google News) failed or returned empty — dropped rather than shipped unverified. Also rejected Hürriyet's Google-News-per-site-search results specifically because roughly half were generic topic-tag pages ("X Latest News, Top Stories") rather than real articles — used the site's own native /rss/turkey path instead, which returned clean, specific headlines throughout.

v1.1: continued the depth pivot, prioritizing a specific sub-case — countries whose ONLY source is state_affiliated=true, meaning zero independent domestic voice at all. Egypt (Al-Ahram, state) gained Mada Masr, a genuinely independent Egyptian outlet publishing substantive critical coverage of state institutions. Also added Mail & Guardian (South Africa, alongside News24) and Nation Thailand (Thailand, alongside Bangkok Post) — both showed thin volume in the standard 24h freshness window at verification time but confirmed as real, actively-publishing outlets via a wider 7-day check (same precedent as round 104). A 4th candidate, Pagina12 (Argentina, to pair with the right-leaning Clarín), had zero results via both native RSS and Google News in 24h and 7d windows — dropped rather than shipped unverified. Qatar (Al Jazeera) and Saudi Arabia (Al Arabiya) remain single-source-and-state-affiliated; no credible independent domestic outlet was identified for either given each country's press environment — flagged for a future round rather than forcing a low-quality addition.

v1.2: closed 3 more depth gaps — Mexico News Daily (Mexico — the prior round's Milenio/Excelsior/Animal Politico/Proceso/Infobae/El Universal attempts had all failed; this English-language outlet aimed at expats/travelers turned out to have real, substantive Mexico-specific reporting), Buenos Aires Times (Argentina, alongside Clarín — succeeded where the prior round's Página12 attempt found nothing), and Vietnam News (Vietnam, state_affiliated=true, alongside the existing state_affiliated=false VnExpress International — mirrors the Turkey TRT/Hürriyet pairing, giving readers both the official state framing and the more independent-leaning coverage for a country where all domestic media operates under Communist Party oversight to some degree).

v1.3: found 3 more single-source-and-state-affiliated countries (same category as Egypt/Qatar/Saudi Arabia from v1.1) and fixed the 3 that had a viable independent candidate: IraqiNews.com (Iraq, alongside the KRG-linked Rudaw), Roya News (Jordan, independent broadcaster, alongside the state-run Jordan Times), Khaleej Times (UAE, private Dubai-based paper, alongside the state-run The National). Rejected Shafaq News and 7iber (empty feeds in both 24h and 7d windows) as Iraq/Jordan alternates before landing on the ones actually used.

v1.4: fixed the inverse of the v1.1-v1.3 gap — countries whose only source is an EXILE/OPPOSITION outlet rather than a domestic one, meaning zero official-state perspective exists (the mirror image of "state-only, no independent voice"). Iran's only source, Iran International, is a London-based opposition broadcaster; added IRNA, Iran's official state news agency (state_affiliated=true), for the government's own framing — same reasoning as the Turkey TRT/Hürriyet and Vietnam News/VnExpress pairings. Also added Naharnet (Lebanon, independent, alongside L'Orient Today) after rejecting 2 other Lebanese candidates: Daily Star Lebanon's Google News results were pure sports-betting-odds spam unrelated to Lebanon, and Al Manar's were entirely generic topic-tag pages — both matched the round-109/117 garbage-result pattern and were dropped.

v1.5: found 3 more single-source-and-state-affiliated countries — Ghana (Graphic Online), Denmark (DR Nyheder), Sweden (SVT Nyheter) — and fixed all 3 with independent alternatives: MyJoyOnline (Ghana), Politiken (Denmark, Danish-language, matching the existing native-language precedent), Dagens Nyheter (Sweden, Swedish-language, same precedent).

v1.6: 3 more single-source-and-state-affiliated fixes — Helsingin Sanomat (Finland, Finnish-language, alongside the state broadcaster Yle), KT Press (Rwanda, alongside the state-run New Times — The Chronicles was tried first but turned out to be a Rwanda/Uganda safari-tourism blog, not a news outlet, and was dropped), Vlast.kz (Kazakhstan, alongside the state-run Astana Times — one of the few genuinely independent Kazakh digital outlets; showed thin volume in the 24h window at verification time, confirmed real via the round-104 wider-window method).

v1.7: ran a systematic scan of every remaining single-source country's state_affiliated flag (rather than spot-checking a handful) and found only 5 left: Angola, New Zealand, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia. Qatar/Saudi Arabia already flagged as likely-permanent gaps. Russia's TASS is offset in practice by the separately-labeled 'Russia (exile, Latvia)' independent source, just split across 2 country-label entries rather than a true single-source gap. Fixed New Zealand: added Stuff.co.nz (private, alongside the public broadcaster RNZ). Angola remains open — Novo Jornal, Expansão, and Valor Económico (via Google News) all returned zero results in this attempt; ANGOP remains the only working Angola feed found so far.

v1.8: back to breadth after the depth pivot — added the registry's first Caribbean, Central American, and Pacific Island sources: Jamaica (Jamaica Observer), Trinidad & Tobago (Trinidad Guardian), Costa Rica (La Nación, Spanish-language), Fiji (The Fiji Times), Papua New Guinea (Post-Courier). Rejected Newsday Trinidad (its feed was entirely legal notices/classified ads, not news) in favor of Trinidad Guardian instead.

v1.9: continued broadening the same 3 regions — Dominican Republic (Diario Libre, Spanish-language), Haiti (Le Nouvelliste, English edition), Samoa (Samoa Observer, thin 24h volume but confirmed real via the round-104 wider-window method). Guatemala (Prensa Libre) and Panama (La Prensa) were attempted but dropped — Prensa Libre's Google News results were bare '- Prensa Libre' placeholders with no headline text, and La Prensa returned zero results via both methods.

v1.10: 5 more first-time countries — El Salvador (elsalvador.com, Spanish), Belize (Breaking Belize News), Barbados (Nation News), Nicaragua (Confidencial, Spanish — an independent/exile-adjacent outlet given Nicaragua's press restrictions under the Ortega government, publishing openly critical coverage including political-prisoner reporting), Bahamas (Eyewitness News — chosen over Tribune 242 after Tribune 242's Google News results included a bare placeholder entry alongside real content).

v1.11: 5 more first-time countries, closing out most of the remaining Caribbean/Central America/Pacific gap list — Honduras (Proceso), Cuba (14ymedio, Spanish — Cuba's leading independent/dissident outlet, largely produced in exile/underground given the domestic press environment; genuinely critical coverage of repression, blackouts, and political prisoners, a meaningfully different voice from the sole state-media alternative Granma), Tonga (Matangi Tonga — some entries are government procurement/tender notices rather than narrative news, but real local news (rugby, community) is present too, a small-market variant of the round-110 'mixed but net-real' case), Solomon Islands (Solomon Star), Vanuatu (Vanuatu Daily Post).

v1.12: retried Panama and Guatemala with fresh candidates after both were dead ends 2 rounds ago (round 124's La Prensa/Prensa Libre attempts both failed) — this time both worked: Panamá América (Panama) and República (Guatemala). This closes out essentially the entire Caribbean/Central America/Pacific gap list opened in round 123; a re-audit of category_context.json (last checked round 108) found it still clean — all entries are generic structural statements with no country-specific framing to fix.

v1.13: back to depth — a 2nd, independent-leaning source for 4 South America/Africa countries that had only a single establishment-leaning outlet: La Silla Vacía (Colombia, independent investigative, alongside the more establishment El Tiempo), Interferencia (Chile, alongside the center-right La Tercera), RPP (Peru, major independent radio/news network, alongside the conservative-leaning El Comercio), Hespress English (Morocco, alongside Morocco World News).

v1.14: added a state perspective to 3 East African countries whose sole source was independent (inverse of the v1.1-v1.3/v1.5-v1.7 gap): Daily News (Tanzania, state, alongside the independent Citizen), New Vision (Uganda, state, alongside the independent Daily Monitor — mixed but net-real, roughly 60% substantive local hard news amid some navigation-page noise in the Google News feed, similar to round 110's Daily Mirror judgment call), EBC (Ethiopia, state, alongside the independent Addis Standard — same mixed-but-net-real pattern). Zimbabwe was attempted (Herald, the state paper) but dropped — zero results in both 24h and 7d windows.

v1.15: 2 more North/Central African depth fixes — APS (Algeria, official state news agency, English, alongside the independent TSA), ActuCameroun (Cameroon, a 2nd independent voice alongside Journal du Cameroun). Zimbabwe retried again (Herald native feed, ZBC News) — both blocked with HTTP 403, a 2nd consecutive dead end; Senegal's RTS and Cameroon's CRTV state broadcasters were also tried and returned only generic station-identity placeholder pages via Google News, not real articles.
